sense.definition: used to describe someone or something that perfectly matches one's tastes, values, or preferences

examples.x: He’s a man after my own heart; he loves hiking as much as I do.
The book you recommended is truly after my own heart.
That restaurant is after my own heart—everything on the menu is healthy.
She’s a teacher after my own heart, always encouraging creativity.
This cozy little café is after my own heart.

sense.definition: someone who believes it is impossible to know whether God exists or not
the typical way a person thinks or feels about something or someone, often affecting their behavior and decisions

examples.x: Her positive attitude towards challenges helped her overcome obstacles and achieve her goals.
A respectful attitude towards others fosters healthy relationships and mutual understanding.
He approached the project with a can-do attitude, inspiring his team to work diligently towards success.
Negative attitudes can limit personal growth and hinder progress in various aspects of life.
A good attitude can make a big difference in team dynamics.

sense.definition: (of a person) believing that the existence of God or supernatural is unknown and unknowable

examples.x: The agnostic philosopher explored questions about the nature of existence and spirituality.
She describes herself as agnostic, as she is unsure whether there is a higher power.
Agnostic philosophers throughout history have grappled with the complexities of existence and the limits of human knowledge.
The agnostic character in the film struggles with the concept of morality in a world without divine guidance.

sense.definition: a way of doing something or dealing with a problem
confident in expressing one's opinions, ideas, or needs in a clear, direct, and respectful manner

examples.x: She made an assertive argument during the meeting, clearly outlining her proposal.
Assertive communication is key in negotiations to ensure your needs are met without being aggressive.
The assertive tone of her voice conveyed confidence and authority.
He admired her assertive personality, which made her stand out in a competitive environment.
Assertive leaders inspire trust and motivate their teams to achieve goals.

sense.definition: to have a particular point of view that is rare and unusual among others

examples.x: Being in a minority of one doesn't make you mad.

sense.definition: something that we think is true or real

examples.x: Many people hold the belief that honesty is the best policy.
The explorer's discovery challenged the prevailing beliefs about the origin of the river.
In some cultures, there are deeply held beliefs about the significance of certain symbols or rituals.
His belief in karma led him to always act with kindness towards others.
He expressed his belief in the importance of education for societal progress.

sense.definition: to hold an opinion that something is the case

examples.x: She believes that art can inspire social change.
They firmly believe that equality is essential for a just society.
I'm starting to believe kindness can truly make a difference.
Our team believes innovation is crucial for success.

sense.definition: to believe something to be the case
having a preference or unfair judgment toward one side or viewpoint over others

examples.x: The journalist's article was criticized for being biased towards a particular political party.
She couldn't be impartial in the debate because of her biased views on the topic.
His biased opinion about the new employee influenced the hiring decision unfairly.
The judge recused herself from the case to avoid any appearance of biased judgment.

sense.definition: to be vastly different from each other in terms of opinions, attitude, etc.

examples.x: Despite being siblings, their personalities were worlds apart - one was outgoing and adventurous, while the other was introverted and reserved.
The political candidates' ideologies were worlds apart, making it challenging to find common ground during the debate.
The two companies' approaches to business were worlds apart - one focused on innovation and risk-taking, while the other emphasized stability and tradition.

sense.definition: too confident or proud in expressing oneself, in a way that is annoying to others

examples.x: She became bumptious after receiving the promotion, constantly bragging about her achievements.
Although she was talented, her bumptious nature often led to misunderstandings.
The bumptious young man interrupted everyone in the room with his unsolicited opinions.
He had a bumptious smile on his face as he boasted about his latest success.
I find his bumptious remarks to be quite off-putting during conversations.

sense.definition: someone whose opinion changes in accordance with the situation
one's views on a specific subject
an agreement reached by all members of a group

examples.x: The team reached a consensus on the new project timeline after extensive discussions.
Consensus among board members was crucial for approving the budget proposal.
The political parties struggled to find consensus on the controversial immigration policy.
Consensus among stakeholders was achieved through compromise and negotiation.
Building consensus among family members was challenging, but they finally agreed on a vacation destination.

sense.definition: the quality of always acting or being the same way, or having the same opinions or standards

examples.x: Successful athletes attribute their achievements to discipline and consistency in training.
The chef's dishes are known for their exceptional flavor and consistency in presentation.
In leadership, consistency is key to building trust and maintaining morale among team members.
The company prides itself on the consistency of its product quality, ensuring customer satisfaction.
Her consistency in academic performance earned her recognition as the top student in the class.

sense.definition: following the same course of action or behavior over time

examples.x: She showed consistent dedication to her studies, earning top grades semester after semester.
The athlete's consistent training regimen led to noticeable improvements in performance.
His consistent kindness towards others earned him the reputation of being a reliable friend.
The teacher praised the student for their consistent attendance and participation in class.
The author's consistent writing schedule allowed them to publish a book every year.

sense.definition: a belief or opinion that is very strong

examples.x: Despite criticism, she held onto her conviction that renewable energy is the key to a sustainable future.
His conviction in the importance of education led him to establish a scholarship fund for underprivileged students.
The artist expressed his convictions through powerful paintings that portrayed themes of equality and justice.
Her deep religious convictions guided her moral decisions and actions throughout her life.
His conviction in the power of education inspired many students to pursue higher goals.

sense.definition: the presence or flow of a set of ideas, feelings, or opinions among a group of individuals

examples.x: There is a strong current of dissatisfaction among employees regarding the new company policies.
The current of anti-immigration sentiment has been growing in certain parts of the country.
A current of optimism swept through the community after the announcement of the new development project.
Ministers are concerned about the current of opposition against the recent tax reforms.
The election campaign tapped into a powerful current of nationalist feelings among voters.

sense.definition: someone who evaluates and provides opinions or judgments about various forms of art, literature, performances, or other creative works

examples.x: Her reviews as a food critic are highly anticipated, as she provides detailed critiques of restaurants and dishes based on taste, presentation, and service.

sense.definition: having clear and definite opinions
(of a person) difficult to fully understand or get close to, often refraining from sharing their true feelings or ideas with others

examples.x: She's always been a deep person, never revealing her true emotions.
Despite working together for years, his colleagues find him deep and mysterious.
He was a deep individual, hiding his feelings behind a calm exterior.
She is a deep person, always contemplating life's big questions, but few know her true thoughts.

sense.definition: having a strong set of beliefs and opinions that are unlikely to change
having a specific feeling, opinion, or attitude about someone or something

examples.x: She was favorably disposed toward the new policy changes.
He seemed poorly disposed to the idea of working late on weekends.
She was cautiously disposed toward the proposed business venture.
They were warmly disposed to their new neighbors.
The board was favorably disposed to the innovative project proposal.

sense.definition: a person who pretends to disagree with an opinion or idea just to promote a discussion concerning a particular subject

examples.x: I know everyone thinks we should support the new zoning proposal, but I'm going to play devil's advocate and say that I think it will have some negative consequences.
The teacher asked the students to play devil's advocate and argue against the author's thesis statement.
The politician was accused of being a flip-flopper, but he defended himself by saying that he was just playing devil's advocate in order to stimulate debate.
The lawyer played devil's advocate during the cross-examination of the witness, trying to poke holes in their testimony.
The reviewer played devil's advocate in their assessment of the film, pointing out its flaws even though they overall enjoyed it.

sense.definition: a belief or a belief system held by an authority who proclaims it to be undeniably true and expects immediate acceptance

examples.x: The church's dogma was strictly enforced, leaving no room for dissenting opinions.
She questioned the dogma of the organization, seeking evidence rather than blind acceptance.
The political leader's dogma was evident in every policy decision made by the administration.
Critics argued that the company's dogma stifled creativity and innovation.
The cult's dogma required followers to adhere to a set of rigid and unquestionable rules.

sense.definition: convinced that everything one believes in is true and others are wrong

examples.x: His dogmatic views on politics made it difficult to have a productive conversation with him.
She was dogmatic in her belief that only her approach to solving the problem was correct.
The leader was known for his dogmatic speeches that left little room for alternative perspectives.
If you keep a dogmatic attitude, you may miss valuable insights from people around you.
After years of experience, he had become less dogmatic and more open to others' opinions.

sense.definition: someone who does not provide a definite answer for a question, particularly when being asked in a poll

examples.x: During the market research, the interviewees were divided into three groups: those in favor, those against, and the don't-knows who remained undecided on the new technology.
In the survey, a sizable percentage of participants fell into the don't-know category when asked about their preferences for the new menu items.
The don't-knows in the focus group highlighted a need for additional information on the proposed changes to the workplace structure.
When asked about his favorite movie genre in the survey, John was a don't-know participant, offering no specific response.

sense.definition: the state in which one holds two opposing ideas at the same time
having firm, unchanging opinions, beliefs, or ideas
very eager to persuade people to accept one's views or opinions

examples.x: The speaker was highly evangelical about the benefits of renewable energy.
Her evangelical enthusiasm for fitness led her to start a health blog.
He adopted an evangelical stance on advocating for animal rights.
Her evangelical commitment to the cause made her a passionate campaigner.
The activist’s evangelical approach aimed to raise awareness about climate change.

sense.definition: a set of words or actions meant to make people accept one's strong views or opinions
a supporter of a theory, belief, idea, etc. who tries to persuade others that it is true or good in order to gain their support

examples.x: He is a leading exponent of renewable energy, advocating for its benefits at every opportunity.
She is considered an exponent of veganism, actively promoting its advantages on social media.
The senator is a prominent exponent of environmental protection laws.
As an exponent of mindfulness, he offers workshops to help others manage stress.
He had been an exponent of free-market capitalism, often debating its merits with critics.

sense.definition: one who makes an abrupt change of opinion, especially a politician
someone who has their own opinions, ideas, and beliefs rather than accepting other people's, especially about religion or politics

examples.x: In an age of conformity, she proudly identified as a freethinker, always striving to think for herself.
As a political freethinker, he often disagreed with both major parties and sought independent solutions.
Despite growing up in a religious community, he became a freethinker, developing his own spiritual beliefs.
The freethinker challenged societal norms, advocating for a more open-minded and progressive approach.
As a freethinker, she never followed the crowd and always questioned traditional beliefs.

sense.definition: (of people or opinions) strong and demanding in manner or expression

examples.x: His forceful leadership style commanded attention and respect from his team.
The forceful delivery of her speech left a lasting impression on the audience.
Her forceful personality often intimidated those around her.
The forceful objection from the opposition stalled the passing of the bill.
His forceful insistence on fairness and equality earned him respect among his peers.
